description_long: This object consists of two side-by-side soprano recorders, each with a cylindrical body and a series of evenly spaced finger holes along the front. The recorders feature a slightly flared bell at the bottom and a beaked mouthpiece at the top, with decorative rings and subtle ridges marking the joints and sections. One recorder is a muted gray color, while the other is a yellowish-gold, both with a smooth, slightly glossy finish. The finger holes are circular and clearly defined, and the mouthpiece area includes a rectangular windway slot. The overall design is simple and functional, with no visible engravings or embellishments. These recorders are typical of those used in educational or casual musical settings and are likely made of plastic.
description: A pair of smooth, cylindrical recorders—one gray and one yellow—each with a beaked mouthpiece, finger holes, and decorative rings, made of plastic.
description_view_0: Front view showing both recorders upright, with all finger holes and mouthpieces clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the cylindrical shape, decorative rings, and the length of both recorders.
description_view_2: Top-down view focusing on the beaked mouthpieces and the windway slots.
description_view_3: Reverse angled view showing the backs and sides of the recorders, with the bell ends prominent.
